hypothesis: Extending the single-objective reactor yield metric to multi-product systems, applying the same closed-loop Bayesian optimization framework to a reactive carbon electrolyzer operating with copper-based catalysts and a mixed bicarbonate-carbonate electrolyte will reveal that a composite reactor yield metric simultaneously weighting CO, C₂H₄, and C₂H₅OH partial current densities against CO₂ utilization converges to an optimum at intermediate bicarbonate-to-carbonate ratios distinct from those optimizing any single product's partial current density, demonstrating that the trade-off landscape governing multi-carbon selectivity and CO₂ utilization is fundamentally non-separable and cannot be predicted from single-product optimization campaigns alone.
